skill.name: maxhub-youtube
skill.description: Integrate with YouTube API through MaxHub proxy to fetch videos, channels, comments, and playlists. Use when users mention youtube, videos, channels, comments, or playlists.
skill.summary: YouTube data integration via MaxHub API proxy
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 168 static findings across 3 files (439 lines). All 117 'external_commands' flags are false positives from markdown code blocks showing curl examples. Network and env_access flags are acceptable API integration patterns. No prompt injection detected. This is a legitimate YouTube data integration skill using MaxHub API proxy.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner Pattern False Positives - External Commands
high_findings.description: 117 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are false positives. The scanner detected backtick syntax in markdown code blocks (```) as shell execution, but these are simply curl command examples in documentation showing API call syntax. No actual shell execution occurs.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code blocks with backtick syntax are documentation patterns, not executable code. The skill contains no shell scripts or command execution.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner Pattern False Positives - Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: HIGH flags for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' are false positives. These are triggered by API path names containing 'get_signed_stream_url' and similar terms. No actual cryptographic code exists in the markdown files.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching on API names triggered false positives. No cryptographic algorithms are present in documentation files.

high_findings.title: High Entropy Strings Are Normal Documentation Content
high_findings.description: Heuristic flags for high entropy (6.53 bits, 6.31 bits) are false positives. The entropy comes from markdown formatting, mixed Chinese/English content, and API response structure examples - not encrypted or obfuscated payloads.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy in markdown files is caused by multilingual content and structured API documentation, not binary/encrypted content.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flags Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: LOW flags for 'system reconnaissance' at lines 308-311 of SKILL.md are false positives. These are standard API documentation mentions of request headers and rate limits, not system exploration commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text discusses API usage guidelines and rate limits, not system reconnaissance. Pattern matching on standard technical terminology caused false positives.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: 5 'Hardcoded URL' flags for aconfig.cn and gitee.com domains are acceptable. These are the documented MaxHub API proxy service endpoints, not malicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate API service endpoints documented in the skill. The skill explicitly restricts network access to the configured MaxHub API endpoint only.

content.user_title: Fetch YouTube Video, Channel, and Comment Data
content.value_statement: Need to search YouTube videos, analyze channel metrics, or extract comments? This skill integrates with YouTube API through MaxHub proxy to fetch comprehensive data without exposing your API credentials.
actual_capabilities: Search videos by keyword with filtering options
Fetch video details including title, description, views, and thumbnails
Retrieve channel information and subscriber counts
Extract video comments and replies (with pagination)
Get trending videos and search suggestions
Access channel videos and shorts content
limitations: Cannot interact with YouTube (likes, comments, subscriptions are read-only)
Rate limited by MaxHub API (60 requests per minute default)
Some data may be restricted by YouTube regional policies
Maximum 5 pages per query to prevent excessive API usage

use_cases.title: Research YouTube content trends for a topic
use_cases.description: Content researchers can search for videos on specific topics, analyze view counts and engagement metrics, and identify popular creators in their niche.

use_cases.title: Monitor competitor YouTube channels
use_cases.description: Digital marketers can track competitor channel performance, analyze video upload patterns, and gather engagement data for benchmarking.

use_cases.title: Collect video comments for sentiment analysis
use_cases.description: Data scientists can extract video comments for natural language processing, sentiment analysis, and audience feedback collection.

prompt_templates.title: Search YouTube videos by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube videos about {topic} and show me the top 10 results with their view counts and channel names.

prompt_templates.title: Get channel statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the subscriber counts and total video views for the YouTube channel at {channel_url}?

prompt_templates.title: Extract video comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the first 50 comments from this YouTube video: {video_url}. Include commenter names and like counts.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze trending content
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current trending YouTube videos in {country} category. For each video, show the title, channel name, view count, and like ratio.

output_examples.input: Search for 'machine learning tutorials' on YouTube
output_examples.output:
Found 10 videos about machine learning tutorials:
1. 'Intro to ML in 2024' by TechAcademy - 125K views, 8.2K likes
2. 'TensorFlow for Beginners' by CodeMaster - 89K views, 5.1K likes
... [7 more results with thumbnails and channel info]

Channel: Example Channel
Subscribers: 1.2M
Total Views: 45.6M
Videos: 234
Created: March 2019
Top Video: 'How to Start Coding' - 2.3M views

best_practices: Use specific keywords for more accurate search results
Check account balance before running batch operations
Configure MAXHUB_BASE_URL to your preferred API endpoint
Use pagination parameters to control data volume
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to auto-generate engagement (likes, comments, subscriptions)
Avoid requesting more than 5 pages in a single query to prevent excessive costs
Do not share your MAXHUB_API_KEY in public channels or documentation

faq.question: How do I get a MaxHub API key?
faq.answer: Register at aconfig.cn, create an account, and generate an API key in your dashboard. Set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads public YouTube data through MaxHub API proxy. It does not access your local filesystem, browser data, or perform any platform manipulation.

faq.question: How many API calls can I make?
faq.answer: Default rate limit is 60 requests per minute. Single queries are limited to 5 pages (about 50 items) to control costs.

faq.question: Can I post comments or like videos with this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads data from YouTube. It cannot perform any write actions like posting comments, liking videos, or subscribing to channels.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my API quota?
faq.answer: The skill will warn you when calling continuously over 20 times. Exceeding quota may temporarily block access to the MaxHub API service.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It can help automate YouTube data collection for your projects.
